ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing An Accounting By Former Administrator
Off Calendar May-23-2023 Continued To Jun-20-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance: Amy Harrington appearing via zoom for and with Glenn Leong (Administrator)
Carol Huey (propria persona) appearing via zoom.

Mrs. Huey explained why she did not appear at the prior two hearings and stated she did not receive a copy of the order re: sanctions. The Court will excuse sanctions at this time. Discussion was held regarding the assets that were escheated to the State, the inability to obtain bank records with respect to the Washington Mutual account, Mrs. Harrington's due diligence and efforts to maintain records, Mrs. Huey not having done very much other than collecting some rental income which was applied to the property expenses, the possibility of obtaining waivers of accounting from all of the beneficiaries, the issue of whether or not Mr. Leong would be able to do a forensic accounting, etc. Status of filing of an accounting by former administrator is continued to June 20, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Any waivers are to be filed by June 13, 2023. The order to appear/order to show cause is off calendar.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: Ateesh Datt. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

Off Calendar Jan-17-2023 Text Ruling

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jan-17-2023 Text Ruling

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing An Accounting By Former Administrator
Off Calendar Jan-17-2023 Continued To Mar-14-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearance in court: Carol Huey (Former Administrator) in propria persona.

The Court stated on calendar before it today are a status on the filing of an accounting by the former administrator and orders to appear and show cause. The Court addressed Ms. Huey stating that even though her duties were handed off to her cousin that she needs to account for the period of time that she served and that the court will need either an accounting or waivers to be filed. Ms. Huey stated that it was handed over to her cousin due to her illness, that she has good days and bad days, that she has medical appointments three times a week, and that she gave everything/the documents for the accounting to the attorney. The Court noted that she may need to get the documents back and provided Ms. Huey with a detailed outline of what the accounting would need to cover such as what was collected, what was spent/paid, and what was handed off to her cousin. The Court also noted that Ms. Huey would need to account from the date of death to the time that the administration was handed over to her Cousin Glenn Leong. Ms. Huey stated that there was real property, that there were retirement funds, that she did not identify the retirement funds, that there was a rentor, that the rentor moved out so that the property could be sold, and that the property was vacant within the last year. Ms. Huey further stated that she was administrator for a little over a year and that her aunt owned only two of the six units. Ms. Huey stated that she collected rent, that it wasn't much, that she paid the bills, and that if the rent did not cover the bills that she would cover the rest. The Court stated that it sounds as if the estate may owe her money and that the people who rented were low income. The Court inquired about the number of heirs. Ms. Huey stated that there are six heirs. Brief discussions were held regarding Ms. Huey either filing an accounting or securing waivers from the other five heirs.

At the conclusion of discussions, the Status Hearing on Filing the Accounting by Former Administrator, Carol Huey, is continued to March 14, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. The accounting or the waivers need to be on file by March 3, 2023. Ms. Huey was advised to appear on March 14th if the accounting or waivers are not on file. The Court vacated the $250.00 sanction order and the order to show cause. Ms. Huey stated that she did not receive the notices in October and November, that she had to go to the doctor on the November date, confirmed that her address is correct, and noted that she doesn't know why she did not receive the notices.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Choi. Matter not reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing An Accounting By Former Administrator
Off Calendar Dec-13-2022 Continued To Jan-17-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person. An Order to Appear will be issued to former Administrator Carol Huey, in propria persona, to appear on Jan-17-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204 to explain why she failed to appear and why an accounting has not been filed. The court orders Carol Huey to pay sanctions in the amount of $250 for failing to appear at today's hearing, payable by noon on Jan-10-2022. Proof of Payment of Sanction shall be filed by noon on Jan-20-2023. An Order to Show Cause why the court should not order Carol Huey to pay further sanctions up to $500 for failure to appear is also set for Jan-17-2023. Written response to the Order to Show Cause is due by Jan-06-2023 with a courtesy copy delivered to the court by close of business on Jan-09-2023. The status hearing is also continued to Jan-17-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-21-2022 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting
Off Calendar Jan-21-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amy Harrington, via ZOOM, for Glenn Leong, Petitioner and Carol Huey, Personal Representative.

The court confirms with Mr. Leong that Winnifred never owned a business and believes that she made a living by working odd jobs. Judge Quinn discloses that he frequented the dry-cleaning business where Winnie and Veronica worked. No objections are presented. The court then questions Veronica's capacity to waive accountings and advises Ms. Harrington that it is hesitant to waive accountings. Ms. Harrington reports that it may be difficult but will prepare an accounting if ordered by the court. The court, counsel, and Mr. Leong discuss the history of this case and why the request for appointment od successor administrator is required. Ms. Harrington advises that she has disclosed the potential conflict of interest to both of her clients and does not believe that any conflict exists that would prevent her from representing Mr. Leong and that a waiver regarding the preceding has been signed by Ms. Huey and Mr. Leong.

After additional discussion, the Accounting Petition and Petition for Letters of Administration are submitted.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: G. Bastain. Not reported.
